tasks_completed: Task 1: Privy integration (done in prior session, commit c991a0f)
Task 2: Build /bet page — useCurrentTournamentMatch hook + BetPage.tsx wired into App.tsx at /bet
tasks_remaining: Task 3: Add claim flow (already integrated in BetPage; verify it works end-to-end — DONE inline)
Task 4: Add QR code to tournaments/viewer.html linking to /bet
Task 5: Faucet endpoint — /faucet in arena sends 0.1 MON to new Privy wallets on first sign-in (capped at 50)
Task 6: Wallet balance display already in BetPage as 'N bets remaining'; done
learnings: Privy embedded wallet uses getWalletClient() from usePrivyWallet hook, not lib/wallet.ts (which only wraps window.ethereum). BetPage calls getWalletClient() from usePrivyWallet directly.
Tournament bracket rounds are in data.bracket.rounds[].matches; find status==='playing' for current match.
BetPage is fully standalone — no Layout wrapper needed since it's a mobile-first fullscreen page, but it is inside Layout via App.tsx route. Consider extracting to a bare route if nav is in the way.
Balance display as 'N bets remaining' uses publicClient.getBalance on the Privy wallet address. Reserves 0.01 MON headroom for gas.
Task 3 (claim flow) is already embedded in BetPage — canClaim check shows 'Claim X MON' button when position.claimable > 0n after resolution.
